metadata.purpose: RAG knowledge base for TARA pipeline — ADAS ECU domain knowledge
metadata.notes: Enriched with AUTOSAR AP functional clusters, ENISA AI threat landscape, SAE J3016/J3061 lifecycle

automation_level.description: Partial to conditional automation — system controls driving but human must supervise or take over on request

ecu_overview.primary_function: Perceives the vehicle environment using sensors, processes data using AI/ML algorithms, and assists or controls driving decisions such as lane keeping, adaptive cruise control, automatic emergency braking, and object detection.
ecu_overview.core_pipeline: Sensors → Perception → Fusion → Planning → Control → Actuation
ecu_overview.operating_environment: Real-time, safety-critical, high compute demand, connected to multiple sensors and vehicle networks
key_characteristics: Connected to CP ECUs for actuation

hardware.mcu: High-performance SoC with hardware accelerator (e.g., GPU/NPU for AI inference)

processing_units.role: Neural network inference for perception — safe hardware acceleration per AUTOSAR AP
Cryptographic operations and key storage — supports ara::crypto

sensors.function: Lane detection, object classification, traffic sign recognition
Object distance and velocity measurement, works in all weather
3D point cloud generation, precise object mapping
Short range obstacle detection for parking assistance
Global positioning for localization and map matching

actuator_interfaces: Ethernet to VCU for drive commands
CAN to steering and braking ECUs (CP ECUs)
communication_interfaces: Ethernet (primary — high bandwidth sensor data, SOME/IP)
CAN (vehicle network commands to CP ECUs)
V2X (vehicle to everything communication — 802.11p or C-V2X)

memory.flash: Large NVM for AI model storage and OTA updates
memory.secure_storage: HSM-protected key vault for cryptographic material
memory.persistent_storage: ara::per managed key-value and file storage for calibration and config

software.os: POSIX-compliant OS (e.g., QNX, Linux with RT patch)

ara_functional_clusters.role: Service-oriented communication between ADAS components and other ECUs
adas_use: Camera ECU to ADAS ECU video stream
Start perception module only when sensors are ready
Restart failed AI model safely without vehicle disruption

ara_functional_clusters.role: Runtime anomaly detection and security monitoring
adas_use: Monitor Ethernet traffic for injection attacks

ara_functional_clusters.role: OTA update handling for ADAS software and AI models
Base C++ APIs and data types for all ADAS applications
adas_use: Safe handling of real-time sensor data processing

ara_functional_clusters.role: Ensures synchronized timestamps across all sensors and ECUs
adas_use: Synchronize camera, radar, LiDAR timestamps for sensor fusion accuracy

ara_functional_clusters.role: Controls access to ADAS services and resources
adas_use: Restrict diagnostic access to authorized sessions only
Control which ECUs can send commands to ADAS

application_layer.function: Fuses sensor data from camera, radar, LiDAR to build environment model
AI/ML neural network inference for pedestrian, vehicle, obstacle detection
Combines outputs of multiple sensors to reduce uncertainty and improve accuracy
Computes safe trajectory based on environment model and vehicle state
Manages ADAS features: AEB, ACC, LKA, LCA, TJA
Processes vehicle-to-everything messages for cooperative driving

stages: Model validation and adversarial robustness testing

ota_update.verification: Cryptographic PKI signature check before apply
ota_update.rollback: Supported — previous version restored on failure

application_lifecycle: Sensor services discovered via Service Discovery
ADAS perception and planning apps start
Decisions sent to VCU via Ethernet

assets.description: Raw camera frames, radar point clouds, LiDAR scans
assets.cybersecurity_property: Integrity and availability are safety-critical — corrupted sensor data leads to wrong driving decisions

assets.description: Trained neural network models for object detection and classification
assets.cybersecurity_property: Model tampering can cause misclassification of objects. Backdoored model can behave maliciously on specific trigger inputs.

assets.description: Labeled datasets used to train the ADAS AI perception models
assets.cybersecurity_property: Poisoned training data causes model to learn incorrect behavior — data poisoning attack vector

assets.description: List of detected and classified objects with position, velocity, type
assets.cybersecurity_property: Corrupted object list directly affects path planning and vehicle control

assets.description: HD map data used for localization and path planning
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered map data leads to wrong navigation and path planning decisions

assets.description: Vehicle location data from GNSS module
assets.cybersecurity_property: Spoofed GNSS data causes wrong localization and navigation decisions

assets.description: ADAS ECU software including OS, middleware, and AUTOSAR AP stack
assets.cybersecurity_property: Unauthorized firmware modification can introduce malicious behavior or disable safety features

assets.description: Sensor calibration parameters and system configuration stored via ara::per
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered calibration degrades perception accuracy systematically

assets.description: Incoming V2X messages from infrastructure and other vehicles (CAM, DENM)
assets.cybersecurity_property: Spoofed V2X messages cause wrong cooperative driving decisions

assets.description: Commands sent to VCU for steering, braking, acceleration
assets.cybersecurity_property: Injected commands can directly cause loss of vehicle control

assets.description: The ADAS SoC including GPU/NPU and HSM
assets.cybersecurity_property: Physical compromise of hardware enables debug port attacks and key extraction

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker uses adversarial patches, laser blinding, or optical illusions to fool the AI model
attack_examples: Adversarial sticker on stop sign causes misclassification

attack_surfaces.description: Radar spoofing using replay of radar signals or false target injection
attack_examples: Phantom vehicle injected causes unnecessary braking
Real obstacle hidden by spoofed signals

attack_surfaces.description: LiDAR spoofing using laser pulses to inject false points or blind the sensor
attack_examples: Fake obstacle injected into 3D point cloud

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker on vehicle Ethernet can inject, replay or intercept ADAS commands and sensor data
Malicious OTA package replaces ADAS firmware or AI models
attack_examples: Backdoored AI model via compromised OTA server

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker broadcasts fake V2X messages to cause wrong cooperative driving decisions
attack_examples: Fake emergency vehicle message causes sudden braking

attack_surfaces.description: UDS over DoIP used to read/write ADAS memory or modify calibration
attack_examples: Extended session unlocked to write calibration
AI model weights read via memory dump

attack_surfaces.description: GNSS spoofing misleads vehicle localization affecting path planning
attack_examples: Fake GPS signals route vehicle wrongly

attack_surfaces.description: Development debug ports left active allow direct memory access and key extraction
attack_examples: JTAG extracts cryptographic keys from HSM
Debug port used to inject code

threat_catalog.title: Camera Adversarial Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses adversarial patches or optical illusions to fool the AI object detection model into misclassifying objects

threat_catalog.title: Radar Signal Spoofing
threat_catalog.description: Attacker replays or synthesizes radar signals to inject phantom objects or hide real ones from ADAS perception

threat_catalog.title: LiDAR Point Cloud Injection
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses laser pulses to inject false points into LiDAR scan, creating phantom obstacles or removing real ones

threat_catalog.title: GNSS Spoofing
threat_catalog.description: Attacker broadcasts fake GPS signals to mislead vehicle localization causing wrong map matching

threat_catalog.title: V2X Message Spoofing — Sybil Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker broadcasts fake V2X messages or Sybil attack creating phantom vehicles to manipulate ADAS cooperative driving

threat_catalog.title: AI Model Backdoor via OTA
threat_catalog.description: Attacker delivers malicious OTA update replacing AI model with backdoored version that misclassifies specific trigger inputs

threat_catalog.title: Training Data Poisoning
threat_catalog.description: Attacker injects malicious samples into training dataset so the model learns incorrect behavior for specific scenarios
threat_catalog.enisa_reference: ENISA_AI_Autonomous_2021 — Data poisoning in AI lifecycle

threat_catalog.title: Sensor Calibration Data Manipulation
threat_catalog.description: Attacker modifies sensor calibration via diagnostic interface causing systematic perception errors

threat_catalog.title: Driving Command Injection via Ethernet MITM
threat_catalog.description: Attacker performs MITM between ADAS and VCU replacing legitimate driving commands with malicious ones

threat_catalog.title: Ethernet Bus Flooding — Real-time Deadline Miss
threat_catalog.description: Attacker floods vehicle Ethernet with garbage traffic causing ADAS to miss real-time sensor processing deadlines

threat_catalog.title: LiDAR Blinding Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses high-power laser to saturate LiDAR sensor, blinding the 3D perception system completely

threat_catalog.title: Sensor Data Replay Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker records and replays old sensor frames to freeze ADAS perception while real conditions change

threat_catalog.title: Sensor Data Eavesdropping
threat_catalog.description: Attacker intercepts Ethernet traffic to extract real-time camera and sensor data enabling surveillance

threat_catalog.title: Diagnostic Session Exploit — Memory Write
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses UDS over DoIP to unlock extended diagnostic session and gain write access to ADAS memory or AI model storage

threat_catalog.title: Debug Port Physical Attack — Key Extraction
threat_catalog.description: Attacker with physical access uses JTAG or debug port from development to extract keys or inject malicious code

security_controls.protects: Ethernet communication between ADAS and VCU
CAN messages from ADAS to safety ECUs
AI model and firmware OTA updates
Runtime monitoring of ADAS network and application behavior
Sensor data integrity on internal IPC and ara::com events
Diagnostic interface and service access from unauthorized ECUs
AI model from adversarial inputs and poisoned training data
Sensor fusion accuracy — prevents timing-based attacks on multi-sensor data

iso21434_tara_context.item_definition: ADAS ECU responsible for environment perception and driving assistance at SAE L2-L3 using AI/ML models and multi-sensor fusion
iso21434_tara_context.item_boundary: Includes camera, radar, LiDAR, GNSS sensors, sensor fusion stack, AI inference engine, path planner, V2X handler, and Ethernet interface to VCU
cybersecurity_goals: Sensor data integrity shall be maintained to prevent wrong driving decisions
AI model integrity shall be ensured to prevent misclassification and backdoor attacks
Driving commands shall be authenticated to prevent command injection
OTA updates shall be cryptographically verified before application
V2X messages shall be authenticated to prevent spoofing-based attacks
Diagnostic access shall be restricted to authorized sessions only

damage_scenarios.description: Loss of vehicle control due to spoofed sensor data causing wrong driving decision
Collision due to AI model misclassifying pedestrian via adversarial attack or backdoor
Vehicle navigated to wrong location due to GNSS spoofing
Fleet-wide ADAS compromise through malicious OTA update with backdoored AI model
ADAS perception disabled due to LiDAR blinding or Ethernet flooding

sae_j3061_lifecycle_phases: Development — security by design, threat modeling
Validation — adversarial robustness testing, penetration testing
Incident response — OTA patch delivery via ara::ucm
relevant_clauses: clause-9 — Item definition and cybersecurity goals
